name: Big Bang
description: The universe is born. All matter, energy, space, and time emerge from a singularity.

name: Earth Forms
description: Our solar system coalesces from a cloud of gas and dust. Earth takes shape.

name: First Life on Earth
description: The earliest single-celled organisms appear in Earth's primordial oceans.

name: Dinosaurs Appear
description: The Triassic period gives rise to the first dinosaurs.

name: Dinosaurs Extinct
description: The Chicxulub asteroid impact wipes out 75% of species, ending the age of dinosaurs.

name: First Humans
description: Homo sapiens emerge in Africa, beginning humanity's long journey.

name: First Cave Paintings
description: Humans create the earliest known art in caves across Europe and Asia — the first cultural preservation.

name: First Australian Stories
description: Aboriginal Australians begin the world's oldest continuous living culture. Their Dreamtime stories encode astronomy, law, and geography across tens of thousands of years — the longest unbroken oral tradition on Earth.

name: Polynesian Navigation Begins
description: Polynesian voyagers set out across the Pacific using stars, waves, and birds to navigate — no instruments, no maps. They settle Hawaii, New Zealand, Easter Island, covering a third of Earth's surface.

name: Agriculture Begins
description: Humans start farming in the Fertile Crescent, beginning civilisation.

name: Kingdom of Kush
description: The Kingdom of Kush rises in Nubia (modern Sudan), rivalling Egypt in power and culture. Its rulers briefly conquer Egypt and rule as the 25th Pharaonic Dynasty.

name: Silk Road Opens
description: Han Dynasty China opens the Silk Road — 4,000 miles of trade routes connecting East Asia to Rome. Ideas, religions, art, and diseases travel alongside silk and spice.

name: Stonehenge Built
description: Neolithic people construct the iconic stone circle in England.

name: Great Pyramid Built
description: Pharaoh Khufu's Great Pyramid at Giza is completed — one of the seven wonders.

name: Trojan War
description: The legendary siege of Troy, immortalised by Homer in the Iliad.

name: Homer writes the Iliad
description: Homer composes the Iliad and the Odyssey, foundational texts of Western literature.

name: Birth of Buddha
description: Siddhartha Gautama is born in what is now Nepal, later to become the Buddha.

name: Birth of Confucius
description: The Chinese philosopher Confucius is born, shaping East Asian culture for millennia.

name: Battle of Marathon
description: The Athenians defeat the Persian army, saving Greek democracy.

name: Parthenon Completed
description: Athens completes the iconic Parthenon temple atop the Acropolis.

name: Alexander the Great Born
description: Alexander III of Macedon is born, destined to conquer much of the known world.

name: Birth of Jesus
description: Jesus of Nazareth is born in Bethlehem. The event that reset the Western calendar.

name: Crucifixion of Jesus
description: Jesus is crucified in Jerusalem, an event that shaped the course of human history.

name: Eruption of Vesuvius
description: Mount Vesuvius erupts and buries Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash.

name: Fall of Rome
description: The Western Roman Empire falls as Romulus Augustulus is deposed.

name: Muhammad Born
description: The prophet Muhammad is born in Mecca, Arabia.

name: Tang Dynasty Golden Age
description: The Tang Dynasty opens China's most cosmopolitan era — poetry, painting, and music flourish. Chang'an becomes the world's largest city and a global crossroads of culture.

name: Great Zimbabwe
description: The stone city of Great Zimbabwe rises in southern Africa — capital of a powerful Shona kingdom controlling the gold trade between interior Africa and the Swahili Coast.

name: Battle of Hastings
description: William the Conqueror defeats King Harold II, forever changing England.

name: Magna Carta Signed
description: English barons force King John to sign the Magna Carta, a landmark in human rights.

name: Mansa Musa's Pilgrimage
description: Mansa Musa, ruler of the Mali Empire and possibly the wealthiest person in history, makes his hajj to Mecca with 60,000 people and 27 tonnes of gold — crashing the Egyptian economy.

name: Black Death Arrives in Europe
description: Bubonic plague reaches Europe, killing up to half the population over the next years.

name: Gutenberg's Printing Press
description: Johannes Gutenberg invents movable type, revolutionising the spread of knowledge.

name: Aztec Empire at its Peak
description: The Triple Alliance establishes the Aztec Empire. Tenochtitlán becomes one of the world's largest cities, with aqueducts, floating gardens, and a population larger than London.

name: Columbus Reaches Americas
description: Christopher Columbus makes landfall in the Bahamas, connecting two worlds.

name: Copernicus Publishes Heliocentric Theory
description: Copernicus proposes the Sun, not Earth, is at the centre of the solar system.

name: Shakespeare Born
description: William Shakespeare is born in Stratford-upon-Avon, England.

name: Galileo Uses Telescope
description: Galileo turns his telescope to the heavens and discovers Jupiter's moons.

name: Newton's Principia Published
description: Isaac Newton publishes the laws of motion and universal gravitation.

name: American Revolution
description: The thirteen colonies declare independence from Britain, founding the United States.

name: French Revolution
description: The French people overthrow the monarchy in a revolution that reshapes Europe.

name: Beethoven's 9th Symphony
description: Ludwig van Beethoven premieres his Ninth Symphony — composed while completely deaf. The 'Ode to Joy' finale becomes humanity's anthem. He never heard a single note of it.

name: Abolition of Slavery (USA)
description: The 13th Amendment abolishes slavery in the United States, ending 246 years of legal enslavement. Four million people are freed.

name: Darwin's Origin of Species
description: Charles Darwin publishes his theory of evolution by natural selection.

name: First Telephone Call
description: Alexander Graham Bell makes the first telephone call to his assistant Thomas Watson.

name: Women's Suffrage — New Zealand
description: New Zealand becomes the first self-governing country in the world to grant all women the right to vote. Kate Sheppard leads a 25,000-signature petition — the largest in the country's history.

name: Wright Brothers First Flight
description: Orville Wright pilots the Flyer for 12 seconds at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.

name: Einstein's Special Relativity
description: Albert Einstein publishes his special theory of relativity, changing physics forever.

name: First Jazz Recording
description: The Original Dixieland Jass Band records the first jazz record in New York. A musical form born from African American culture in New Orleans reaches the world.

name: Titanic Sinks
description: The RMS Titanic strikes an iceberg and sinks on its maiden voyage.

name: World War I Begins
description: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand triggers the Great War.

name: World War II Begins
description: Germany invades Poland. Britain and France declare war. The world is at war again.

name: Hiroshima Atomic Bomb
description: The United States drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan.

name: Indian Independence
description: India gains independence from British rule after a non-violent resistance movement led by Mahatma Gandhi. The largest democracy on Earth is born.

name: DNA Double Helix Discovered
description: Watson and Crick describe the double helix structure of DNA.

name: Sputnik Launched
description: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite.

name: First Human in Space
description: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space aboard Vostok 1.

name: MLK — 'I Have a Dream'
description: Dr Martin Luther King Jr delivers his 'I Have a Dream' speech to 250,000 people at the Lincoln Memorial — the defining moment of the American civil rights movement.

name: JFK Assassination
description: President John F. Kennedy is assassinated in Dallas, Texas.

name: Moon Landing
description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin become the first humans to walk on the Moon.

name: Māori Arrive in Aotearoa
description: Polynesian navigators arrive in Aotearoa New Zealand, completing humanity's final great migration. They name the islands and develop a rich culture of whakapapa (genealogy), waiata (song), and carving.

name: Mandela Released
description: Nelson Mandela walks free after 27 years in prison, becoming a symbol of the global anti-apartheid movement and paving the way for democratic elections in South Africa.

name: World Wide Web Invented
description: Tim Berners-Lee proposes the World Wide Web at CERN.

name: Berlin Wall Falls
description: The Berlin Wall falls, ending the Cold War division of Europe.

name: Human Genome Sequenced
description: The Human Genome Project completes the first full sequence of human DNA.

name: iPhone Released
description: Apple releases the first iPhone, transforming human communication.

name: First Black Hole Image
description: The Event Horizon Telescope captures the first ever image of a black hole.

name: James Webb Telescope Launched
description: NASA launches the James Webb Space Telescope, the most powerful ever built.
